title: Amparoa cordata
intro: Amparoa cordata, also known as Chilean tree daisy, is a flowering plant species native to the Andes Mountains in South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Amparoa cordata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Amparoa cordata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve coughs, sore throats, and respiratory congestion.
